customer_goal: Increase the useful simultaneous-camera capacity of the vectorized custom Gaussian renderer without changing its rendering equation, output contracts, Isaac integration, or CUDA correctness.

verdict.summary: Reusing the CUB sort scratch allocation as compact depth-bucket storage saves exactly 150,994,944 bytes on Home B8, 67,108,864 bytes on Garage B8, and 754,974,720 bytes on Home B40. The exact 240-view Home B40 case still passes every output, motion, cache, and overflow check while driver-reported process memory falls from 22,485 MiB to 21,765 MiB. Strict 72-view custom-versus-gsplat fidelity, focused CUDA/Kit/Fabric tests, and Compute Sanitizer memcheck all pass.
verdict.performance_interpretation: This is a capacity and headroom optimization, not a demonstrated rendering-speed improvement. Unprofiled throughput moved by roughly one percent in the favorable direction, while the fresh Nsight kernel-time diagnostic moved by 0.27 percent in the unfavorable direction. Both are treated as run-to-run noise because no kernel or launch order changed.
verdict.capacity_interpretation: B40 is validated on this L4 protocol. B48 remains outside physical capacity: after observing 575,647,560 emitted intersections, bounded growth requested a 20,908,169,815-byte backend workspace and the allocator could not place its 6.50 GiB sort-scratch allocation alongside the scene, outputs, Isaac, and prior partial allocations.
verdict.customer_interpretation: The change creates real memory headroom for vectorized Gaussian observations, but it does not yet prove safe co-residency with a substantial RTX workload, 24 Hz batch latency, an L40 result, or any replacement for RTX rendering.

source.retained_change: The compact depth_bucket_tau tensor is a typed contiguous view over the leading bytes of sort_temp. The shared allocation is sized to the larger logical requirement, and workspace accounting distinguishes unique physical storage from logical tensor views.
source.telemetry_change: Capacity allocation and Isaac trajectory evidence report physical bytes, logical bytes by tensor, unaliased requested bytes, exact alias savings, and the installed storage-alias map.

lifetime_argument.ordering: On projection-cache misses, depth-bucket accumulation and cutoff construction finish before direct intersection emission and the emitted-prefix radix sort on the same current CUDA stream. The fast path also synchronizes that stream before invoking CUB. Projection-cache hits skip both depth accumulation and sorting.
lifetime_argument.allocation_rule: physical shared bytes = max(CUB sort scratch bytes, depth-bucket tau bytes)
lifetime_argument.accounting_rule: Unique untyped-storage pointers are counted once; depth_bucket_tau therefore contributes zero additional physical bytes while remaining visible in logical accounting.

protocol.coverage: Every source view appears exactly once in each reported acceptance case.
protocol.custom_path: Headless Isaac Sim MinimalRendering, USD/Fabric CUDA camera ingestion, compact projection cache, emitted-prefix global radix ordering, output-contract validation, and artifact bookkeeping.
protocol.fidelity_reference: Pinned gsplat contract-exact replay of the persisted Garage B8 trajectory using the same deterministic screen-space EWA equation family.
protocol.warmup: Shader, JIT, extension, adaptive-capacity, and renderer warmup occur outside acceptance timing.
output_requirements: foreground and background semantic policy valid
zero visible or intersection capacity overflow

stress_limit.classification: Expected capacity boundary. The request was below the configured backend-only workspace ceiling but did not leave enough physical memory for the scene, outputs, Isaac runtime, and allocation placement.

profiling.case: Home B8 exact moving trajectory at 384x384
profiling.scope: Full-process trace with two warmup batches and 30 measured batches. Adaptive retry work and warmup remain in the kernel-time numerator; 256 rendered warmup-plus-measured views are used for the per-view diagnostic.
profiling.diagnosis: The 0.27 percent profiled difference conflicts in sign with the roughly one-percent unprofiled gain and is classified as noise. Global radix sorting remains the largest runtime and memory target.

finite_gpu_ladder.custom_backend_smoke: pass, including pointer alias and physical-versus-logical accounting assertions
finite_gpu_ladder.deterministic_cuda_smoke: pass, including bitwise repeatability, capacity invariance, and equal-depth Gaussian-ID ordering
finite_gpu_ladder.kit_extension_smoke: pass with CustomCudaBackend on cuda:0, compact projection cache, and Fabric Scene Delegate enabled
finite_gpu_ladder.fabric_camera_smoke: pass with CUDA output and zero-copy Torch handoff
finite_gpu_ladder.multi_scene_cuda_smoke: pass with defined inactive-camera background outputs
finite_gpu_ladder.projection_cache_cuda_smoke: pass with coherent cache events, CUDA outputs, zero overflow, and 2,097,152 exact alias-savings bytes

next_optimization.target: Reduce the emitted-intersection sorting working set rather than making further small dense-array changes.
next_optimization.candidate_experiment: Evaluate CUB's DoubleBuffer SortPairs API using the already allocated key/value ping-pong buffers, record which buffer owns the final sorted values, and measure whether CUB temporary storage shrinks without adding a correctness or cache-coherence copy.
next_optimization.important_prior_negative: Merely making the existing pointer-API scratch query mode-aware was already tested in issue 28 and saved zero bytes on this nondeterministic path. The DoubleBuffer experiment is a different hypothesis and must be measured before any claim.

limitations: B40 still uses 21,765 MiB of driver-reported process memory on a 23,034 MiB L4. This creates more headroom than the base but is not proof of safe co-residency with a substantial Isaac RTX workload.
B48 fails physical allocation even though the requested backend workspace is below the configured 20 GiB ceiling; that ceiling is backend-only and is not a whole-process memory guarantee.
All reported batches miss the 41.67 ms per-batch latency target; throughput per image and synchronous observation latency are different metrics.
The throughput deltas versus the base are not claimed as speedups because the code changes allocation only and the profiled and unprofiled deltas disagree in sign.
No L40 result is included. The result must be rerun on the intended L40 before making L40-specific customer claims.
The strict fidelity gate compares the same screen-space EWA equation family. OVRTX perspective fidelity and RTX compositing remain separate questions.
Nsight Systems timing is diagnostic and includes adaptive retry and warmup work. No Nsight Compute hardware-counter claim is made.

evidence.availability: Raw artifacts are intentionally ignored by Git; checksums bind this committed record to the preserved local evidence.
